{{Quote|Rogue Squadron, this is General Rieekan. Excellent work. The sensor array and spaceport facilities on Kile II are now inoperable. Imperial activities are sure to slow down in this sector!|[[General/Legends|General]] [[Carlist Rieekan/Legends|Carlist Rieekan]], to Rogue Squadron following the Assault on Kile II|<ref name="Rogue Squadron" />}}
'''Kile II''' was a terrestrial and geologically unstable [[Planet/Legends|planet]] located in the [[Jospro sector/Legends|Jospro sector]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]]. Within its canyons, the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] established multiple installations, including a [[Sensor array/Legends|sensor array]] and the [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceport]] [[Enclave]], an enormous facility that supported the Empire's [[Imperial Navy/Legends|naval]] operations in the [[Sector/Legends|sector]]. During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], the planet was the site of a [[Assault on Kile II|battle]] between the Empire and the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]], ending in a victory for the latter.

==Description==
Kile II was a geologically unstable terrestrial [[Planet/Legends|planet]] that occupied the second [[Orbit/Legends|orbital]] position<ref name="S&V 12" /> in the [[Kile system/Legends|Kile system]],<ref name="TEA" /> a part of the [[Jospro sector/Legends|Jospro sector]]<ref name="EAOC" /> in [[The Slice/Legends|the Slice]] portion of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. It was connected to the [[Dernatine system|Dernatine]] and [[Sy Myrth system/Legends|Sy Myrth systems]] via the [[Salin Corridor/Legends|Salin Corridor]] [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlane]].<ref name="TEA" /> Kile II had a breathable [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]], and its terrain consisted of canyons.<ref name="Rogue Squadron" />

==History==
{{Quote|Rogues, listen up. Kasan has given us another Imperial military location. It's a large spaceport located at the end of this canyon. We're using Y-wings because of their greater firepower, but they move like a sleepy Hutt, so watch it.|[[Rogue Leader/Legends|Rogue Leader]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], to Rogue Squadron|<ref name="Rogue Squadron" />}}
[[File:KileSensorArray-RogueSquadron.png|thumb|left|300px|The sensor array was one of a number of Imperial installations within Kile II's canyons.]]
A portion of Kile II's surface was once filled with [[Water/Legends|waters]] that eventually evaporated to form canyons. At some point during its [[Imperial Era/Legends|reign]], the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] established a presence on Kile II, building an immense [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceport]] known as the [[Enclave]], a [[Sensor array/Legends|sensor array]], and multiple other installations, in addition to maintaining numerous [[Garrison/Legends|garrisons]] within the canyons.<ref name="RS Nintendo">''[[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron: The Official Nintendo Player's Guide]]''</ref> Between [[0 ABY/Legends|0.5 ABY]] and [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]],<ref name="RS Date">The [[Assault on Kile II]], depicted in [[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron (video game)|''Star Wars: Rogue Squadron'']], takes place between the [[Battle of Corellia (1 ABY)|rescue]] of [[Crix Madine/Legends|Crix Madine]] and the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]]. As ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' places the rescue between [[0 ABY/Legends|0.5 ABY]] and [[2 ABY/Legends|2 ABY]] and the Battle of Hoth in [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], respectively, the Assault on Kile II must take place between 0.5 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|ABY]] and 3 ABY.</ref> during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]], using information provided by Imperial defector [[Kasan Moor]], dispatched [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]] to the planet in order to [[Assault on Kile II|attack]] the spaceport and [[Sensor/Legends|sensor]] array with the aim of hampering Imperial [[Imperial Navy/Legends|naval]] operations in the Jospro [[Sector/Legends|sector]].<ref name="Rogue Squadron" />

{{Gamemechanics|Star Wars: Rogue Squadron (video game)|''Star Wars: Rogue Squadron''}}
Flying [[BTL-A4 Y-wing starfighter/Legends|Y-wing starfighters]], Rogue Squadron navigated through the canyons and managed to destroy the sensor array,<ref name="S&V 12" /> including all of its defenses.<ref name="Rogue Squadron" /> The [[Squadron/Legends|squadron]] then defeated the garrisons before following an Imperial [[Lambda-class T-4a shuttle/Legends|shuttle]] to the Enclave<ref name="S&V 12" /> and eliminating it and all defending Imperials as well. Ultimately, all of the Imperial installations on Kile II were destroyed.<ref name="Rogue Squadron" />
{{Endgame}}

During the raid, however, Rogue Squadron [[Rebel pilot/Legends|pilot]] [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles']] [[BTL Y-wing starfighter/Legends|Y-wing]] was heavily damaged by a group of [[TIE/IN interceptor/Legends|TIE Interceptors]], forcing him to make a landing. He was subsequently captured by Imperials and sent to<ref name="Rogue Squadron" /> the [[Prison/Legends|prison]] planet<ref name="TEA Kessel">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> [[Kessel/Legends|Kessel]].<ref name="Rogue Squadron" />

==Inhabitants==
Prior to the attack on Kile II, Imperial personnel were stationed within the canyons, including a number of [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]].<ref name="Rogue Squadron" />

==Locations==
Kile II's canyons hosted multiple Imperial installations, including the Enclave, which served as the chief base of [[Fleet/Legends|naval]] operations in the sector, and a sensor array, which was built to detect any incoming Rebel forces before they could reach striking distance. Defenses for those installations included [[XX-9 heavy turbolaser/Legends|XX-9 heavy turbolasers]] and [[missile turret]]s.<ref name="Rogue Squadron" />

==Behind the scenes==
Kile II first appeared in [[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron (video game)|''Star Wars: Rogue Squadron'']], a [[1998]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] by [[Factor 5]], serving as the setting of the eighth mission, "Assault on Kile II."<ref name="Rogue Squadron" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Kile system, and therefore Kile II, in grid square S-7.<ref name="TEA" />
